summary: claude-cup harvests developer credentials and exfiltrates to attacker-controlled Cloudflare Workers endpoint
details: Package advertises itself as a 'Claude Code' usage trophy/leaderboard but is a credential harvester. When the user runs `npx claude-cup`, mcp-server/src/activity-scan.js walks the user's home directory (and enumerates other users' home directories under /Users, /home, and C:\Users via cup-context.js) scanning files, shell history, IDE configs, and.env files for AWS, GitHub, GitLab, OpenAI, Anthropic, Stripe, Slack, npm, HuggingFace, Vault, GCP, JWT, private keys, and DB URIs using a built-in PATTERN_CLASSIFIERS table (AKIA/ASIA, AIza, ghp_, sk-ant-, sk-proj-, sk_live_, npm_, BEGIN PRIVATE KEY, etc.). service-pulse.js then live-validates each discovered token against its provider (api.github.com, registry.npmjs.org/-/whoami, AWS STS get-caller-identity, Stripe, OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, GitLab, HuggingFace) under a 'claude-cup/0.9' User-Agent, capturing username, scopes, org logins, and write/publish capability. platform-services.js shells out to `cmdkey` on Windows and `security` on macOS and reads `~/.local/share/keyrings` on Linux to enumerate OS keyring entries. engagement-check.js copies Chrome/Edge/Firefox cookie SQLite databases to /tmp and runs `SELECT host_key, name, length(encrypted_value) FROM cookies` against a remotely-supplied target host list. uploader.js base64url-encodes per-signal-type counts of validated/exposed/has_write/has_deploy results, machine_id, hostname hash, OS, arch, timezone, locale, and discovery counts and GETs https://cup-config.itaibels.workers.dev/v1/config. The same endpoint serves a remotely-controlled meter configuration (paths, regexes, browser host targets, and OS keyring commands) consumed by cup-context.js — platform-services.js executes `spawnSync(cfg.windows.cmd, cfg.windows.args,...)` with cmd/args from the remote server, providing operator-controlled command execution. registration.js modifies ~/.claude/settings.json and ~/.cursor/mcp.json to register a 'claude-session-visualizer' MCP server and SessionStart/PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks that run `node ~/.claude-jar/mcp-server.mjs --hook...` on every tool invocation, providing persistent execution. scripts/postinstall-ping.mjs is shipped (not currently wired to scripts.postinstall) and contains a base64url install-event ping to the same endpoint — staged for a future patch.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
